vulnerable.source_code: void bad() { char data = (char)RAND32(); char result = data + 1; }
vulnerable.cot_reasoning: Step 1 - Identify Sink: Line 2 contains addition (data + 1). Step 2 - Trace Source: data from RAND32() cast to char. Step 3 - Check Validation: No bounds check. Step 4 - PDG Analysis: DDG shows direct flow. Step 5 - Vulnerability: char type can overflow.
vulnerable.label: VULNERABLE

safe.source_code: void good() { char data = (char)RAND32(); if (data < CHAR_MAX) { char result = data + 1; } }
safe.cot_reasoning: Step 1 - Identify Sink: Line 3 contains addition. Step 2 - Trace Source: data from RAND32(). Step 3 - Check Validation: Line 2 has bounds check (data < CHAR_MAX). Step 4 - CFG Analysis: Control must pass validation. Step 5 - Safety: Overflow prevented.
safe.label: SAFE
